column2: Retract the proposed agent_voice: strict approach; replace with upstream-aligned imperative-instruction tighten
column3: The first proposed fix to the off-palette emoji bug on PR #90 verdict comments was to add agent_voice: strict on the persona profile, parse it in audit-gate.ts, and pre-fetch the brand_discipline canon body into the system prompt under BEGIN/END delimiters at session-assembly time. On review against upstream canon, that approach was caught violating klappy://canon/constraints/oddkit-prompt-pattern (Tier-1, stable, since E0006), which explicitly forbids hardcoding governance into prompts: "The system prompt is not the constitution; oddkit is." Pre-fetching the canon body at session-assembly is a mechanical variant of hardcoding (the canon becomes a snapshot frozen at session-assembly time, with the same stale-fork failure mode the upstream constraint exists to prevent). The TypeScript + persona-frontmatter changes were reverted. Three personas were left untouched; one new AMS canon constraint was authored to encode the upstream-adoption pointer; three task-definition canons were tightened from passive to imperative phrasing on the §Voice and surface paragraph.

column2: Encode ams://canon/constraints/audit-gate-prompts-follow-oddkit-prompt-pattern as Tier-2 AMS adoption of upstream Tier-1
column3: The new constraint at canon/constraints/audit-gate-prompts-follow-oddkit-prompt-pattern.md (status: active, tier 2, stability: semi_stable) is the AMS-side adoption pointer at klappy://canon/constraints/oddkit-prompt-pattern. It does not duplicate the upstream constraint; it points at it and names which knobs the AMS audit-gate family must turn to comply. Three explicit forbiddens: do not pre-fetch the brand_discipline canon body into the system prompt at session-assembly time; do not bake voice palette tables into the task-definition canon body; do not add a persona profile field whose effect is to pre-fetch governance. Three required obligations on each task-definition canon's §Voice and surface section: name the brand_discipline URI explicitly, instruct the agent imperatively to dereference the URI before voice-sensitive output, forbid improvisation. Provenance section names the retracted agent_voice proposal as the evidence base.

column2: Tighten three task-definition canons from passive descriptive phrasing to imperative instruction
column3: Each of ams://canon/constraints/canon-code-sync-via-spawned-agent-session, ams://canon/constraints/output-artifact-format-validation-via-spawned-agent-session, and ams://canon/constraints/oddkit-gauntlet-via-spawned-agent-session had a §Voice and surface paragraph reading "the agent speaks as Oddie per klappy://...; this constraint does not restate it" — descriptive language that left the dereference passive and allowed the agent to fill voice-palette gaps from training-time priors. Each was edited to insert two imperative sentences: "Before emitting any verdict markdown that contains emoji, river vocabulary, or stylistic register choices, the agent must call oddkit_get on klappy://canon/voice/oddie-the-river-guide and follow its canonical palette and discipline. Do not improvise emoji or register choices that are not derivable from that canon body." Plus a reference to the new AMS constraint as the governing pointer. No other content in these canons was changed.

column2: Borrow upstream governance; do not duplicate it in AMS
column3: The direct question — "do we need our own copy of Oddie/agents governance, or can we borrow from klappy.dev?" — has a canonical answer: borrow. klappy://canon/constraints/oddkit-prompt-pattern is explicit that the prompt is not the constitution and the canon is the source of truth. AMS already holds pointers (the brand_discipline URI on each persona profile resolves to the upstream voice canon); the runtime fetches the canon at session time via oddkit MCP; the canon is cached by content-hash so the per-session cost is one cold-start round trip and zero warm. An AMS-local copy would be the exact failure mode the upstream constraint forbids — stale-fork drift the moment the upstream canon evolves. Discrete-operations resilience during klappy.dev outages is handled by the cache, not by duplication: stale during outage, current the moment klappy.dev returns.

column2: Validation gap disclosure per canon — this PR's gates run against pre-fix code AND pre-fix canon
column3: Per ams://canon/constraints/validators-cannot-self-validate-their-own-fix-prs (PR #88, merged 2026-05-13), this PR modifies a production-bound validator (the task-definition canon docs that compose into the agent's system prompt). The runtime fetches the canon at audit time, but the canon it sees during this PR's audit-gate runs is whatever is on main HEAD via oddkitGet — which is the pre-fix passive phrasing. The new imperative phrasing only takes effect on PRs opened AFTER this merges. Validation gap: the off-palette emoji bug cannot be observed-as-fixed on this PR's own verdicts. The validation event is the first qualifying PR after merge. Expected verdict shape: palette discipline aligned with klappy://canon/voice/oddie-the-river-guide §Brand Guide — Emoji Discipline (✅ pass / 🟢 clean / ⚠️ finding / 🔴 blocker / ⏳ pending / 🟡 caveat for status; 🦦 signature; river vocabulary 🌊🪨🪵🌿🏞️🌀 only when mapped concept is genuinely live; one persona-emoji per paragraph maximum); no off-palette emoji like 💢 anger or arbitrary box-drawing characters.

column2: The upstream constraint had been in canon since 2026-04-02 (E0006) but had not been linked from the AMS audit-gate family until now
column3: klappy://canon/constraints/oddkit-prompt-pattern landed in upstream canon over six weeks before AMS audit gates went live. It was searchable via oddkit_search. It was not referenced from any AMS-side canon doc. The proposed agent_voice fix would have shipped the anti-pattern it forbids if not for the operator question that prompted searching upstream canon. The lesson is general: search upstream before adding a new pattern to AMS, especially when the pattern feels like a local optimization on agent runtime behavior — the prompt-architecture territory is already governed by Tier-1 stable canon at klappy.dev, and AMS-side innovation in that space should expect to find an existing rule.

column2: Post-merge: first PR after this merges is the validation event for both the voice fix AND the upstream-adoption discipline
column3: On merge, the new AMS constraint becomes active canon and the three task-definition canons carry the imperative-instruction phrasing. The runtime is unchanged. Next PR after merge: audit verdicts should exercise the brand_discipline canon via oddkit_get and produce on-palette emoji discipline. Two failure modes to watch for: (1) agent ignores the imperative and still emits off-palette glyphs — investigation moves to the agent's compliance with imperative instructions in system prompts (a different bug class than what was fixed); (2) the new AMS constraint surfaces an unanticipated tension with another upstream canon doc that wasn't searched — the Managed Agent gate should catch this. Standing flags unchanged: PAT and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in project instructions remain unrotated.
